text.title: Stakeholder Engagement (Advanced) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the stakeholder-engagement-advanced methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Classify stakeholder level from behavioural evidence only (attendance, response time, public statements, decisions made). Never classify from assumed intent.
rule.rationale: Polite emails read as Neutral; actual engagement is revealed by action, not tone.

rule.statement: Move stakeholders one level at a time. Never plan a jump from Resistant directly to Leading — earn each step with a concrete commitment.
rule.rationale: Skipping levels produces shallow engagement that collapses under first pressure; Resistant stakeholders who feel manipulated regress further.

rule.statement: Build the Engagement Assessment Matrix with columns: Stakeholder, Power (H/M/L), Interest (H/M/L), Current Level, Desired Level, Gap (+/-), Next Activity.
rule.rationale: The matrix makes engagement gaps visible in one view and drives prioritisation — high power + high gap = highest project risk.

rule.statement: Apply contact cadence by quadrant: Manage Closely weekly; Keep Satisfied bi-weekly; Keep Informed weekly broadcast; Monitor monthly summary.
rule.rationale: Over-communicating to low-interest stakeholders trains them to ignore messages.

rule.statement: Flag a stakeholder as drifting when last recorded interaction exceeds the cadence threshold for their quadrant with no recorded blocker reason.
rule.rationale: Drift is invisible without a system; a stakeholder Supportive 3 months ago may now be Neutral.

list.item: One-off small tasks with no organisational politics
Adversarial negotiations (procurement, legal disputes) — engagement implies collaboration
Crisis communications — different discipline, different cadence and tone
Projects under 4 weeks with a single decision-maker
